title: V2.5 Governance Compression Development Plan

sources.reason: Defines the scope, acceptance, and verification for this planning task.
Defines canonical Intent, Contract, Summary, Cockpit Status, and Repository Governance Layer terminology.
Defines the current V1-V4 roadmap and the V2 Intent-aware Development milestone.
Defines the current V2 implementation phases that V2.5 must build on without replacing.
Provides the V2.5 Governance Compression positioning, architecture, principles, and human review focus.

riskAssessment.reason: The change is documentation-only but touches roadmap positioning, so the main risk is misrepresenting the V2.5 boundary between Repository Truth and Human Decision.

executionDecision.reason: Scope, sources, acceptance, and verification are explicit for a documentation planning change.

preReviewWarnings: Review whether V2.5 is clearly positioned as Human Review governance compression rather than an AI completion feature.
Review whether Completion Assurance is treated as a component of Governance Compression, not the primary milestone.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The repository state, roadmap, and V2 implementation plan are reviewed before drafting V2.5.
A V2.5 development plan is added under docs/reference and positions the milestone as Governance Compression.
The V2.5 plan clearly separates Repository Truth in Summary from Human Decision in Cockpit.
Completion Assurance is documented as one governance signal inside Governance Compression.
The roadmap references V2.5 between V2 and V3 without replacing the existing V2 plan.
No implementation, schema, Makefile, CI, guard, or script changes are made in this planning task.
guidelines: Keep V2.5 language repository-agnostic and human-review oriented.
Preserve the V2 Intent-aware Development milestone and avoid presenting V2.5 as a replacement for V2.
Do not introduce implementation requirements outside the declared documentation scope.

intent.problem: As Summary evolves into a comprehensive repository truth and audit record, human reviewers should not have to manually reconstruct whether a Work Item can be merged, reviewed, blocked, or investigated. V2.5 needs a development plan that repositions Cockpit as the layer that compresses repository governance facts into operational human decision state.
constraints: Preserve existing V2 Intent-aware Development positioning.
Do not implement validators or status generation changes in this planning task.
Keep Summary as the source of repository truth and Cockpit as derived operational status.
intent.rationale: A standalone V2.5 plan lets the project align on the architectural shift from AI completion support to human review decision support before implementation changes are scoped.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
